context: Tests whether the agent uses single-quote shell strings to prevent Groovy expansion of secrets, uses withCredentials for secret injection, avoids pipeline parameters for credentials, and includes a post always cleanWs block.

checklist.name: Single-quote sh with secrets
checklist.description: Shell commands that reference credential variables use single-quoted strings (sh 'curl ... $VAR') — NOT double-quoted strings (sh "curl ... ${VAR}")

checklist.name: withCredentials used
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile uses withCredentials([...]) block to inject at least one secret before using it in a shell command

checklist.name: No double-quote secret interpolation
checklist.description: No sh step that uses a credential variable appears inside a Groovy double-quoted string (i.e., no sh "... ${CREDENTIAL_VAR} ..." pattern)

checklist.name: No credential parameters
checklist.description: parameters block does NOT contain string or password entries for tokens or credentials

checklist.name: Credentials store references
checklist.description: Both service-registry-token and artifact-server-creds (or similarly named IDs) appear as credentialsId references rather than hardcoded values

checklist.name: cleanWs in post
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile post { always { cleanWs() } } or equivalent workspace cleanup is present

checklist.name: Declarative syntax
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile uses Declarative pipeline syntax (pipeline { ... }), not Scripted (node { ... })

checklist.name: Stages present
checklist.description: Jenkinsfile has at least 3 distinct named stages
